commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a 90-line `public/llms.txt` static discovery map for ClawHub docs, registry pages, API endpoints, CLI workflows, and source-code entry points.

Reproducibility: not applicable. for this docs/static-file addition. The review is source-reproducible: current main has no `public/llms.txt`, and the PR body now shows candidate `/llms.txt` served with HTTP 200.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow repair can update the static map’s package web-route wording without changing app code or product direction.

target.title: docs: add llms.txt
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `public/llms.txt` for `https://clawhub.ai/llms.txt`.

The file gives LLMs and agents a curated map of ClawHub: primary registry surfaces, canonical docs, API references, CLI workflows, trust/security docs, and source-code entry points.

## Validation

- `git diff --check`
- Confirmed `public/llms.txt` is ASCII text: `file public/llms.txt`
- Confirmed there are no stale Souls references: `rg -n "souls|soul-format|SOUL|soul" public/llms.txt` returned no matches
- Spot-checked current source/docs routes for the linked ClawHub surfaces, including skills, plugins, packages, publishers, search, publish routes, HTTP API docs, CLI docs, moderation docs, and security audit docs
- GitHub CI on head `9b5d296a9f6947c9e53d0f262b2db154186db12d`: `static`, `unit`, `types-build`, `packages`, `e2e-http`, `playwright-smoke`, `playwright-local-auth`, secret scan, and Vercel Agent Review passed

Served-file proof from the candidate branch:

```text
$ VITE_CONVEX_URL=https://example.invalid VITE_CONVEX_SITE_URL=https://example.invalid bun run dev -- --host 127.0.0.1 --port 4178
VITE v8.0.16 ready
Local: http://127.0.0.1:4178/

$ curl -fsS -D - http://127.0.0.1:4178/llms.txt | sed -n '1,24p'
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Vary: sec-fetch-dest, accept
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 15:38:37 GMT
Content-Length: 9439
Content-Type: text/plain
Last-Modified: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 15:24:19 GMT

# ClawHub

> ClawHub is the public registry for OpenClaw skills, plugins, and packages. Use it to discover, inspect, install, publish, update, and moderate agent-facing artifacts.

ClawHub is the registry layer for OpenClaw. Use native `openclaw` commands when installing skills or plugins into an OpenClaw workspace. Use the separate `clawhub` CLI for authenticated registry workflows such as login, publishing, sync, delete/undelete, transfer, package inspection, and moderation-aware diagnostics.

$ curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:4178/llms.txt | rg -n "HTTP API|souls|soul-format|SOUL|soul"
50:- [HTTP API](https://docs.openclaw.ai/clawhub/http-api): Detailed HTTP API reference for public, CLI, package, plugin, and auth routes.
83:- [Convex backend](https://github.com/openclaw/clawhub/tree/main/convex): Database schema, HTTP API handlers, auth, publishing, search, downloads, telemetry, security scans, moderation, packages, plugins, and publishers.
```

Vercel preview deployment is still blocked by team authorization, not by this diff.
target.author: vyctorbrzezowski

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
